title: sida carrascoana
intro: Sida carrascoana, also known as Carrasco's sida, is a plant species in the mallow family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Sida carrascoana include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Sida carrascoana can be prepared as an infusion or decoction to make herbal tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
